authority_notice.text: Dashboard cards and graph views are projections, not a source of truth; backend state, graph, queue, and provenance ledgers remain authoritative.

navigation.label: Overview
navigation.summary: Shows current best hypotheses, unresolved risk, and next research action.

navigation.label: Hypothesis board
navigation.summary: Separates current best hypotheses from challengers and retired stale claims.

navigation.label: Graph explorer
navigation.summary: Displays a backend graph projection with provenance and conflict badges.

navigation.label: Run timeline
navigation.summary: Shows run lifecycle events, backend state update status, and follow-up actions.

overview.title: Codex auth boundary

authority_notice.text: Dashboard cards and graph views are projections, not a source of truth; backend state, graph, queue, and provenance ledgers remain authoritative.

overview.topic_summary: Tracks whether scheduled research can safely reuse a Codex session when freshness and workspace signals diverge.

current_best_hypotheses.title: Fail closed on workspace drift
current_best_hypotheses.summary: Scheduled execution should stop when backend inspection cannot confirm the configured workspace root.
current_best_hypotheses.why_currently_best: It preserves principal and workspace isolation while the session healthcheck path is still being validated.

active_conflicts.summary: Freshness failure can mean recoverable token age or security-relevant identity drift.
active_conflicts.why_it_matters: The dashboard must not summarize the topic as simply healthy while this ambiguity is unresolved.

status_indicators.label: Active conflict
status_indicators.summary: One unresolved conflict blocks treating the stale-session challenger as safe.
status_indicators.next_visible_action: Open the graph explorer or run timeline before retrying the queue item.

status_indicators.label: Queue health
status_indicators.summary: One queued run remains available after session preflight succeeds.
status_indicators.next_visible_action: Worker may claim queue_001 after backend preflight.

next_research_actions.label: Attack stale-session challenger
next_research_actions.reason: The current best hypothesis depends on distinguishing recoverable staleness from identity drift.
next_research_actions.command_hint: crb run start topic_codex_auth_boundary --input "counterargument: warning-only stale sessions may be safe" --json

authority_notice.text: Dashboard cards and graph views are projections, not a source of truth; backend state, graph, queue, and provenance ledgers remain authoritative.

cards.title: Fail closed on workspace drift
cards.summary: Stop scheduled execution when backend inspection cannot confirm the configured workspace root.
cards.next_visible_action: Compare against the warning-only stale session challenger.

cards.title: Warning-only stale session path
cards.summary: A stale but account-verified session might be safe to defer without blocking the queue.
cards.next_visible_action: Gather support and challenge evidence before retry policy changes.

cards.title: Reuse every ChatGPT session
cards.summary: Older claim retired because it lacks principal, workspace, and freshness boundaries.
cards.next_visible_action: Keep visible as stale context; do not use as current best.

authority_notice.text: Dashboard cards and graph views are projections, not a source of truth; backend state, graph, queue, and provenance ledgers remain authoritative.

nodes.label: Fail closed on workspace drift
nodes.summary: Current best hypothesis for scheduled execution safety.

nodes.label: Warning-only stale session path
nodes.summary: Challenger hypothesis retained for adversarial validation.

nodes.label: Stale session versus identity drift
nodes.summary: Unresolved conflict blocks treating stale sessions as safe.

nodes.label: Session inspection fixture
nodes.summary: Backend-owned fixture proving principal and workspace checks are first-class contract fields.

authority_notice.text: Dashboard cards and graph views are projections, not a source of truth; backend state, graph, queue, and provenance ledgers remain authoritative.

events.title: Run queued
events.detail: Queue item selected the stale-session challenger for attack.

events.title: Current best challenged
events.detail: The run compared fail-closed workspace drift against warning-only stale-session handling.

events.title: Conflict retained
events.detail: The unresolved conflict remained visible because evidence did not separate staleness from identity drift.

events.title: Backend update applied
events.detail: Backend graph update was accepted and the next frontier action was recorded.

next_actions.label: Schedule adversarial verification
next_actions.reason: The conflict remains unresolved after the completed run.

state_snapshots.title: Loading backend snapshot
state_snapshots.message: The dashboard is waiting for a backend-owned topic snapshot before showing research state.
state_snapshots.next_visible_action: Keep the view in a loading state until the backend snapshot response arrives.

state_snapshots.title: No hypotheses yet
state_snapshots.message: The topic has no accepted backend hypothesis projection; do not invent placeholder conclusions.
state_snapshots.next_visible_action: Prompt for topic bootstrap or show queued bootstrap work.

state_snapshots.title: Graph projection unavailable
state_snapshots.message: The graph projection failed validation, so the dashboard must hide graph content until backend repair succeeds.
state_snapshots.next_visible_action: Show backend repair guidance and preserve the authority notice.

state_snapshots.title: Run is dead-lettered
state_snapshots.message: The run reached terminal queue failure and needs explicit backend retry or repair before it can continue.
state_snapshots.next_visible_action: Link to the dead-letter detail endpoint and show retryability.

state_snapshots.title: Queue claim is stale
state_snapshots.message: A worker claim exceeded the stale threshold; this is not healthy queued work.
state_snapshots.next_visible_action: Show recover-stale or dead-letter recovery actions mediated by the backend.
